2604.27410.title: From Unstructured to Structured: LLM-Guided Attribute Graphs for Entity Search and Ranking
2604.27410.paper_summary: Entity search, i.e., finding the most similar entities to a query entity, faces unique challenges in e-commerce, where product similarity varies across categories and contexts. Traditional embedding-based approaches often struggle to capture nuanced context-specific attribute relevance. In this paper, we present a two-stage approach combining Large Language Model (LLM)-driven attribute graph construction with graph-aware LLM ranking. In the offline stage, we extract structured product attributes from unstructured text, and construct a reusable attribute graph with category-aware schemas. In the online stage, we rank retrieved candidates by reasoning over this structured representation rather than raw text, reducing per-product token usage by 57% while improving ranking precision. Experiments show that our approach outperforms multiple baselines under zero-shot scenarios, achieving a over 5% improvement in average precision without requiring training data, generalizes robustly across diverse product categories, and shows immense potential for real-world deployment.
2604.27410.paper_summary_zh: 實體搜尋，即尋找與查詢實體最相似的實體，在電子商務中面臨獨特的挑戰，因為產品相似性在不同類別和情境中有所不同。傳統的基於嵌入的方法常常難以捕捉細微的情境特定屬性相關性。在本文中，我們提出了一種兩階段的方法，結合了大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的屬性圖構建和圖感知的LLM排序。在離線階段，我們從非結構化文本中提取結構化產品屬性，並構建一個具有類別感知架構的可重用屬性圖。在在線階段，我們通過對這一結構化表示進行推理來對檢索到的候選者進行排序，而不是使用原始文本，從而將每個產品的標記使用量減少了57%，同時提高了排序精度。實驗表明，我們的方法在零樣本場景下超越了多個基準，實現了平均精度超過5%的提升，且不需要訓練數據，並在各種產品類別中具有強大的泛化能力，顯示出在現實世界部署中的巨大潛力。
2604.27410.author: Yilun Zhu et.al.
2604.27410.authors: Yilun Zhu, Nikhita Vedula, Shervin Malmasi
